response:
THE DESIGNER IS WORKING ON A PROJECT INVOLVING A PROMOTIONAL POSTER FOR AN EXHIBITION. THE POSTER INCLUDES EIGHT CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. THE CENTRAL CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES CAN BE CALCULATED AS THE SUM OF THE INDIVIDUAL AREAS. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS GIVEN BY πR². THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R, THE SECOND IS 1.1R, THE THIRD IS (1.1)²R, AND SO ON UP TO THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH IS (1.1)⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS π[R² + (1.1R)² + (1.1)⁴R² + ... + (1.1)¹⁴R²]. THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH A COMMON RATIO OF (1.1)². THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS πR²[1 - (1.1)¹⁶]/[1 - (1.1)²]. THIS EXPRESSION REPRESENTS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES.

FOR THE FIFTH CIRCLE, THE RADIUS IS (1.1)⁴R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1)⁴R. THIS CIRCUMFERENCE IS USED TO PLACE TEXT CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH. TO FIND THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS, DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY 0.5. THE RESULT MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π(1.1)⁴R. DIVIDING THIS BY 0.5 GIVES 4π(1.1)⁴R. THIS VALUE MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE GREATEST INTEGER LESS THAN OR EQUAL TO 4π(1.1)⁴R. THE DESIGNER MUST ENSURE THAT THIS NUMBER IS AN INTEGER AND THAT THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THE RESPONSE. THE ANSWER IS THE MAXIMUM INTEGER VALUE THAT SATISFIES THESE CONDITIONS.
